jokes.text: Almost always, yeah. You know, Maeby's never around anymore... and Ann's off with her religious group, trying to get Nip/Tuck off the air.
jokes.setup: Michael asks if George Michael is alone
jokes.punchline: George Michael's matter-of-fact delivery about being perpetually alone with specific absurd reasons
jokes.explanation: Perfect George Michael character moment - deadpan delivery of his loneliness with specific absurd details about Ann's crusade

jokes.text: Something about God wants people to age naturally- I don't know. Ironically, she likes Gangy.
jokes.setup: Ann's religious group opposes Nip/Tuck because God wants natural aging
jokes.punchline: The irony that she likes Gangy (Lucille), who has clearly had extensive plastic surgery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic observation delivered with George Michael's innocent obliviousness. The economy is perfect - maximum comedy in minimal words.

jokes.text: You don't have to do that. - Great, because I didn't do it this time.
jokes.setup: Michael says he'd never date anyone without George Michael being okay with it
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately admits he already did exactly that
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael self-serving behavior - asking permission retroactively with perfect comedic timing

jokes.text: Her? - Didn't she turn out to not be blind though? - Exactly.
jokes.setup: Michael mentions dating Maggie Lizer again
jokes.punchline: Michael's 'Exactly' response to her not being blind, as if that's the point
jokes.explanation: Great callback that rewards viewers who remember the Maggie storyline. Michael's 'Exactly' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: But she's filled out a little bit. [Narrator] Michael hadn't seen her in eight and a half months... during which she had filled out. Really? Could have sworn it was a year.
jokes.setup: Michael says Maggie has 'filled out a little bit'
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals she's very pregnant, then Michael's confused timeline math
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual misdirection enhanced by Michael's oblivious math skills. Classic AD technique of undercutting dialogue with narrator.

jokes.text: See, that's what I thought. - Boy, you really are my kid. - Good. Apparently, so is this one.
jokes.setup: Father and son bonding over suspicion of Maggie lying
jokes.punchline: Michael's deadpan revelation that he's apparently the father
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation from bonding moment to plot revelation. Shows Michael's tendency to bury the lede.

jokes.text: to collect and test her urine. - It was there that they stumbled upon a fat suit. - That's what it is.
jokes.setup: Narrator explains the convoluted plan to test Maggie's urine
jokes.punchline: They find a fat suit instead, delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Absurdist escalation of the spy mission. The casual 'That's what it is' reaction to finding a fat suit is perfectly understated.

jokes.text: Oh, you're so right, Michael, 'cause I'm fakin' it- - But he was proven wrong. - Not.
jokes.setup: Maggie sarcastically admits to faking pregnancy
jokes.punchline: She adds 'Not' after Michael leaves, revealing it was sarcasm
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection using the 'Not' construction. Simple but effective way to fool both Michael and the audience.

jokes.text: Hello? - [Screaming] Mom, you've got to stop starting conversations like that.
jokes.setup: Lucille answers phone with dramatic screaming
jokes.punchline: Michael's exasperated reaction to her habitual dramatics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille character moment. Her dramatics are so consistent that Michael has learned to expect them.

jokes.text: Who remembers about what? - [Narrator] I do.
jokes.setup: Lucille dismissively asks who remembers their fight
jokes.punchline: Narrator breaks fourth wall to say he remembers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where the narrator becomes a character. Perfect economy and subverts expectations about omniscient narration.

jokes.text: Up yours, granny! - You couldn't handle it!
jokes.setup: George Sr. test drives a Ford Escape
jokes.punchline: He yells insults at Lucille while speeding away
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag of George Sr.'s childish rebellion. The insults are perfectly petty for his character.

jokes.text: Yeah, the Bronco's been discontinued. We're trying to shed that whole 'fugitive on the run' thing. - This is the Escape. - What a fun name, hmm?
jokes.setup: Salesman explains they discontinued the Bronco to avoid fugitive associations
jokes.punchline: He offers the 'Escape' to an actual fugitive who finds it appealing
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic setup where the salesman's solution creates an even more appropriate problem. The layered irony is perfectly constructed.

jokes.text: George Michael, you're going to have to be the baby of the family. - Um, Maeby's younger than me.
jokes.setup: Lucille dramatically declares George Michael must be the new baby
jokes.punchline: George Michael points out basic logic that Maeby is actually younger
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Lucille's selective logic and George Michael's earnest literalism creating comedy through contrast.

jokes.text: Not Dr. Wordsmith. - How's my son? - He's going to be all right.
jokes.setup: Doctor delivers what seems like good news about Buster
jokes.punchline: Sets up the 'all right' vs 'alright' pun
jokes.explanation: Setup for the iconic 'all right' pun. Lucille's 'Not Dr. Wordsmith' adds character-specific dread.

jokes.text: You're never going in the ocean! Never! - We're here to see him. - You're my baby! I'm never letting you go!
jokes.setup: Hospital waiting room scene with family drama
jokes.punchline: Lucille physically fights Michael for possession of George Michael
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy escalation showing Lucille's possessiveness taken to absurd extremes. Good character work.

jokes.text: Yes. He's lost his left hand, so he's going to be 'all right.'
jokes.setup: Family celebrates thinking Buster is okay
jokes.punchline: Doctor explains the literal meaning - he'll be 'all right' because he lost his left hand
jokes.explanation: One of the most famous puns in television history. Perfect structure, dark humor, and became a cultural reference. The build-up and payoff are flawless.

jokes.text: You son of a bitch! I hate this doctor! - He's a very literal man.
jokes.setup: Lucille explodes in rage at the doctor's pun
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's massive understatement about his literalness
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up to the pun. Lindsay's understatement contrasts beautifully with Lucille's rage.

jokes.text: Actually, I'm relieved. I don't have to go to army because the seal with the bow tie attacked me. I feel like I've lost three pounds.
jokes.setup: Buster should be traumatized by losing his hand
jokes.punchline: He's relieved and focused on the weight loss benefit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster character moment - finding the silver lining in losing a limb. The weight loss comment is absurdly practical.

jokes.text: My hat is off to you. - And my hand is off to you.
jokes.setup: Michael uses the common expression 'my hat is off to you'
jokes.punchline: Buster mirrors it with the literal reality of his missing hand
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that turns a common expression into dark humor. Buster's cheerful delivery makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: I mean, a seal didn't bite your Linus off, did it? - No, a seal didn't bite my Linus off.
jokes.setup: Buster asks if Michael lost his 'Linus' (penis) to a seal
jokes.punchline: Michael's deadpan confirmation that his penis is intact
jokes.explanation: Buster's innocent innuendo using 'Linus' as a euphemism. Michael's literal response adds to the awkwardness.

jokes.text: Then go to her. Ask for her hand. - Yeah. And, uh, maybe... I'll get one for my brother while I'm at it.
jokes.setup: Buster tells Michael to 'ask for her hand' in marriage
jokes.punchline: Michael suggests getting a literal hand for Buster
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay turning the romantic cliche into literal problem-solving. The callback to Buster's condition is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Get him out of here! - Hmm? - Get him out of here! Get the- [Bleeps] Out of my room!
jokes.setup: Buster was previously cheerful about his condition
jokes.punchline: He suddenly snaps and violently demands Michael leave
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Buster's emotional instability. The delayed reaction to Michael's insensitive joke works well.

jokes.text: Have you seen Maggie? It's kind of important. - Oh, you just missed her. She's in the bar. What a spanking I gave her in court today.
jokes.setup: Michael asks about Maggie's whereabouts
jokes.punchline: Barry's inappropriate innuendo about 'spanking' her in court
jokes.explanation: Classic Barry Zuckerkorn inappropriate language. His obliviousness to the sexual undertones is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Go get 'em. - He spanked me! - Did you spank her? - No!
jokes.setup: Barry encourages Michael while Maggie arrives angry about being 'spanked'
jokes.punchline: The confusion over literal vs figurative spanking
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the spanking misunderstanding with Michael caught in the middle of the confusion.

jokes.text: Of course. That's what the fat suit is for.
jokes.setup: Barry explains Maggie bought a fat suit for her client
jokes.punchline: Michael realizes this explains the fat suit Lindsay and Tobias found
jokes.explanation: Good callback that connects plot threads. Michael's matter-of-fact realization is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: [Spanks] - Ooh, I did good today.
jokes.setup: Barry continues talking about his court victory
jokes.punchline: He literally spanks himself in celebration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barry moment - his complete lack of self-awareness about his spanking obsession made physical.

jokes.text: That's great. He's probably some geek Simpsons writer's kid.
jokes.setup: Maggie reveals the father is from Harvard via sperm bank
jokes.punchline: Michael's dismissive comment about Simpsons writers (show creators are from Harvard)
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke since AD's creators are Harvard-educated comedy writers. Michael's dismissiveness is ironically elitist.

jokes.text: And while you're at it, let's all give me a big hand!
jokes.setup: Buster returns home from the hospital
jokes.punchline: He asks for 'a big hand' (applause) despite having lost his hand
jokes.explanation: Another excellent hand pun from Buster. His cheerful delivery of dark humor is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Guess this means you don't wanna play 'got your nose' with me.
jokes.setup: Family reacts uncomfortably to Buster's condition
jokes.punchline: Buster suggests playing a children's game that involves pretending to remove body parts
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark joke connecting a children's game to his actual amputation. Shows Buster's coping mechanism through humor.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! What-What happened to your- He's got no hand!
jokes.setup: Gob arrives and sees Buster
jokes.punchline: His delayed, obvious observation delivered with shock
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob reaction - stating the obvious with dramatic flair. His shock is both genuine and self-centered.

jokes.text: You're back in the real world. You're not gonna be hand-fed anymore.
jokes.setup: Flashback to Gob releasing his trained seal
jokes.punchline: He tells the seal it won't be 'hand-fed' while literally feeding it a hand
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant double meaning - the seal literally won't be hand-fed because it now prefers actual hands. Perfect setup for Buster's attack.

jokes.text: How the hell would a seal know how to tie a bow tie anyway? - I can barely tie the yellow one that I wear in my act. - This one had a yellow bow tie!
jokes.setup: Lucille questions how a seal could wear a bow tie
jokes.punchline: Gob accidentally reveals he uses yellow bow ties, matching Buster's description
jokes.explanation: Great escalating reveal as Gob accidentally incriminates himself. The color match is the perfect smoking gun.

jokes.text: Oh, Mom. After all these years, God's not gonna take a call from you.
jokes.setup: Lucille blames herself for praying for God to take something from them
jokes.punchline: Lindsay points out God probably has caller ID for Lucille by now
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to Lucille's history of being awful. The modern technology metaphor for divine rejection is perfect.

jokes.text: Well, first of all, I'm sure there are writers on The Simpsons who aren't geeks.
jokes.setup: Michael's dismissive comment about Simpsons writers
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's defense of comedy writers (while being one herself)
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-follow up to Michael's comment. Lindsay inadvertently defending the show's own creators.

jokes.text: Is he trying to get out of having this baby? - We want this child. It's given us a reason to stay together.
jokes.setup: Lindsay accuses Michael of trying to avoid responsibility
jokes.punchline: She and Tobias reveal they want Maggie's baby to save their marriage
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Lindsay and Tobias's delusions about their relationship and parenting.

jokes.text: Where's your child? - I don't have a child, Michael. - We don't have a child, Michael. - That's why we want-
jokes.setup: Michael asks about their child
jokes.punchline: Lindsay and Tobias both deny having a child, then realize they mean Maeby
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work showing how self-absorbed they are that they temporarily forget their own daughter exists.

jokes.text: She's with her debate club, and they're on their way to Sacremende for the semifinals. [Narrator] She wasn't. And a Google search of the word 'Sacremende' only came up with this.
jokes.setup: Tobias confidently states Maeby's whereabouts
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals it's false and that 'Sacremende' isn't even a real word
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection revealing how little the parents know about Maeby. The Google search gag adds modern tech humor.

jokes.text: It's another Bluth Boy adventure. We're like a team. - We're like Red McGibbon and Bullet. - Who?
jokes.setup: Michael tries to bond with George Michael using adventure metaphors
jokes.punchline: George Michael has no idea what Michael is referencing
jokes.explanation: Good generation gap humor showing Michael's dated references. George Michael's confused 'Who?' is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: You didn't even go inside. - It was taped to the door. - It's probably the Sparkletts guy.
jokes.setup: George Michael appears to have completed the mission instantly
jokes.punchline: He found the evidence without even going inside, and Michael deflates it further
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimax humor. Michael's dismissive explanation shows his tendency to rationalize away evidence.

jokes.text: Close call there, Bullet. I'm so sorry about that.
jokes.setup: They nearly get caught by police during their 'investigation'
jokes.punchline: Michael apologizes for the 'adventure' he dragged George Michael into
jokes.explanation: Callback to the earlier adventure reference. Michael's guilt over endangering his son is played for comedy.

jokes.text: I'm lookin' for the seal that's got the taste for kitty blood.
jokes.setup: Gob arrives at the dock to find his seal
jokes.punchline: He incorrectly says 'kitty blood' instead of 'mammal blood'
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob malapropism. His confusion of 'mammal' with 'kitty' is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: God! It's like herding cats.
jokes.setup: Gob is literally trying to use cats to catch a seal
jokes.punchline: He uses the expression 'herding cats' while literally herding cats
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literalization of the common expression. Gob is actually herding cats while complaining about it being like herding cats.

jokes.text: Uncle Gob? Maeby?
jokes.setup: Gob's cat chaos intersects with Maeby's film shoot
jokes.punchline: Family members unexpectedly encounter each other during their separate schemes
jokes.explanation: Good convergence of separate plotlines showing the interconnected family chaos

jokes.text: There's a father. There's two fathers. David! - I'm making gravy.
jokes.setup: Taylor dramatically reveals there are two fathers
jokes.punchline: Carter's mundane response about making gravy
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between dramatic revelation and domestic mundanity. Shows the couple's dynamic perfectly.

jokes.text: Hot cops [Hisses] Hot
jokes.setup: Michael tracks down the address
jokes.punchline: The 'hot cop' stripper answers the door with his signature line
jokes.explanation: Standard callback to established running gag - functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Well, why should you? I just hope you find out which one of you can breast-feed.
jokes.setup: The couple explains they don't want to know which is the biological father
jokes.punchline: Michael makes an inappropriate joke about male breastfeeding
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate humor creating awkwardness. His tendency to make the wrong joke at the wrong time.

jokes.text: What are you doing? - Okay, so she's fat. - Your Honor, this is an outrage! - Wow. Did I lose this one.
jokes.setup: Barry tries to prove Loretta isn't fat by removing her fat suit
jokes.punchline: His plan backfires spectacularly and he immediately admits defeat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barry moment - his incompetence leads to immediate self-awareness of failure. The escalation is beautifully handled.

jokes.text: Oh, no! My water just broke! - Quickly, Your Honor. Quickly throw it out.
jokes.setup: Loretta's water breaks during the trial
jokes.punchline: Barry thinks she means throw out the case, not rush her to hospital
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misunderstanding as Barry interprets a medical emergency as legal strategy. Perfect Barry logic.

jokes.text: Look, this is kind of weird advice for me to be giving... but why don't you just tell him the truth? I mean, he'll respect you for it. - Thanks, Maeby. - Okay, now I'm just lying for no reason.
jokes.setup: Gob gives sincere advice about telling the truth
jokes.punchline: Maeby points out that she's been lying about everything to him
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta moment where Maeby acknowledges the absurdity of the situation. The irony is beautifully constructed.

jokes.text: Lord, let Buster forget everything. And if you need to take another hand to make that happen... please make it Gob's.
jokes.setup: Lucille prays for Buster to forget his trauma
jokes.punchline: She volunteers Gob's hand as sacrifice without hesitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille moment showing her favoritism and casual cruelty. The prayer format makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: Oh! No, stop it, stop it, stop it! Oh, you caught my tendon in your claw! - My claw? Aw, God! [Hysterical Whimpering] I'm a monster!
jokes.setup: Buster wakes up and grabs something with his hook
jokes.punchline: He realizes he's using a mechanical hand and has an existential crisis
jokes.explanation: Great escalation as Buster realizes the horror of his situation. His dramatic reaction is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Oh! No, stop it, stop it, stop it! Oh, you caught my tendon in your claw! My claw? Aw, God! I'm a monster!
jokes.setup: Buster forgets he lost his hand and tries to use it
jokes.punchline: Realizes he has a claw and thinks he's become a monster
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Buster's psychological trauma manifesting

jokes.text: Don't smoke, sonny. [Wheezing Cough]
jokes.setup: Old man gives health advice
jokes.punchline: He immediately has a terrible smoker's cough
jokes.explanation: Standard ironic health advice joke. Gets the job done but not particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: Is this our little bundle of... two gay cops' baby?
jokes.setup: Michael looks at the newborn baby
jokes.punchline: He awkwardly tries to be politically correct about the gay parents
jokes.explanation: Michael's awkward attempt at progressive language. His discomfort with the situation creates mild comedy.

jokes.text: Boy, you just cannot get off the breast thing, can you?
jokes.setup: Michael continues making breastfeeding references
jokes.punchline: Taylor calls out Michael's obsession with the topic
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Michael's earlier inappropriate joke. Shows his tendency to repeat awkward humor.

jokes.text: So l- Hey, I outsourced it, okay?
jokes.setup: Maggie explains how she handled the pregnancy she couldn't carry
jokes.punchline: She uses corporate buzzword 'outsourced' for surrogacy
jokes.explanation: Clever use of business terminology for personal matters. Shows Maggie's lawyer mentality applied to pregnancy.

jokes.text: [Narrator] But 'M' didn't stand for 'main floor.'
jokes.setup: George Michael gets on elevator and presses 'M' for main floor
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals 'M' stands for morgue
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection gag with dark humor. The narrator's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: When we're together, it is like- - Magic? - I was gonna say 'train wreck.'
jokes.setup: Michael starts to describe their relationship romantically
jokes.punchline: He deflates Maggie's hopeful 'Magic?' with 'train wreck'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Michael's brutal honesty. The contrast between 'magic' and 'train wreck' is perfect.

jokes.text: [Narrator] But that, too, turned out to be a lie.
jokes.setup: Michael and Maggie vow no more lies
jokes.punchline: Narrator immediately reveals that vow itself was a lie
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending narrator intervention. The irony of lying about not lying is beautifully executed.

jokes.text: But unfortunately, you've still got a hook in your ass.
jokes.setup: Doctor explains Gob's hook has been removed with no damage
jokes.punchline: Reveals Buster's hook is now stuck in Gob's rear end
jokes.explanation: Great visual payoff to the earlier attack. The doctor's clinical delivery of absurd information is perfect.

jokes.text: You have got to be kidding me.
jokes.setup: Maggie encounters something shocking (likely the Hot Cops)
jokes.punchline: Her exasperated reaction to another absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction beat. Gets the job done but nothing special. Maggie's exasperation is relatable but not particularly clever.
